hypothesis: Using a continuation strategy that optimizes against progressively sharpened powder patterns—starting with heavily broadened Pseudo-Voigt peaks (inflated Caglioti U, V, W) and annealing them to nominal values—within symmetry-projected gradient descent will reduce spurious local minima along symmetry axes and improve StructureMatcher recovery by at least 20 percentage points compared with fixed-width losses on the MP20 subset under 0.05–0.1 lattice noise.
